items.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active with major model and product announcements in early 2026. In February, they released Claude Opus 4.6 (February 5) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (February 17), with Opus 4.6 positioned as their flagship model for agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance — featuring a 1M token context window in beta. Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier performance for coding and professional work at scale. Both models are available on claude.ai, the Claude API, and major cloud platfor

items.title: Claude Developer Platform - Claude API Release Notes
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Claude Sonnet 4.6 is the latest balanced model offering improved agentic search performance with fewer tokens consumed, supporting extended thinking and a 1M token context window (in beta). Claude Haiku 3 (claude-3-haiku-20240307) is being deprecated with a retirement date of April 19, 2026, with migration to Claude Haiku 4.5 recommended. The web search tool and programmatic tool calling are now generally available with no bet

items.title: Anthropic Says Claude Can Now Use Your Computer to Finish Tasks | CNBC
items.author: www.cnbc.com
items.snippet: Anthropic had a highly active March 2026 on X/@AnthropicAI. The biggest story was the accidental leak of 'Claude Mythos,' a new model described as a 'step change' in capability that is currently in limited early-access testing. Leaked internal documents warned the model could significantly heighten cybersecurity risks by rapidly finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities — a disclosure that rattled markets, including crypto. Anthropic confirmed the model's existence after the leak, noting i

items.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the economic front, they released a labor market impact study (March 2026) presenting a new measurement framework for AI's effect on employment, finding limited evidence of displacement so far. Separately, the Anthropic Economic Index series continues with a February 2026 'Learning Curves' report and a January 2026 'Economic Primitives' report that introduced new metrics for understanding how Claude is actually
